records.ety: [L. obtundere, obtusum; ob (see Ob-) + tundere to strike or beat. See Stutter.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Obtunded; p. pr. & vb. n. Obtunding.]

defs: To reduce the edge, pungency, or violent action of; to dull; to blunt; to deaden; to quell; as, to obtund the acrimony of the gall.
quotes: They . . . have filled all our law books with the obtunding story of their suits and trials.
